competitors.product_name: Epic Agent Factory + Chart with Art
key_features: Agent Factory: no-code visual builder to create and deploy AI agents across clinical workflows
Chart with Art: ambient listening for outpatient, now expanding to bedside nursing and home care
Curiosity foundation models: Epic-trained clinical LLMs
85%+ of Epic customer base actively using AI tools
Deep EHR integration (native — it IS the EHR)
Genomics and interoperability announcements at HIMSS26
competitors.target_market: Large US health systems (250+ bed hospitals)
competitors.pricing_model: Bundled with Epic EHR license (est. $500K-$5M+ implementation, per-provider monthly fees)

latam_presence.details: No significant LATAM presence. Some academic medical centers in Brazil use Epic but adoption is minimal. Cost prohibitive for LATAM clinics.

strengths: Dominant US market position (>35% of US hospital beds)
Massive training data from billions of clinical encounters
No-code agent builder lowers customization barrier
weaknesses: Extremely expensive — inaccessible to small/medium clinics
No LATAM market presence or localization
Closed ecosystem — no interoperability with non-Epic systems

threat_to_holilabs.reasoning: Epic serves large US hospitals at $500K+ implementations. HoliLabs targets LATAM primary care clinics at 1/100th the cost. Different market, different language, different regulatory environment.

key_features: Ambient documentation with ~1.5 minute post-visit processing
Contextual Reasoning Engine for clinically useful and billable notes
Source-mapped AI summaries (clinicians can verify against transcript)
28+ languages supported
50+ specialties covered
Outpatient + ED + inpatient coverage
#1 Best in KLAS 2026 for Ambient AI (2nd year)
Projected 80M+ patient conversations in 2026
competitors.target_market: US health systems (academic medical centers, safety-net hospitals, community health)

latam_presence.details: No LATAM operations. 28+ languages includes Spanish but no Portuguese clinical NLP. No LATAM billing code support.

weaknesses: US-focused — no LATAM regulatory compliance (LGPD, ANVISA, ANS)
No billing code support for TUSS/CBHPM/CUPS

threat_to_holilabs.reasoning: If Abridge enters LATAM (possible given 28+ languages), their ambient quality is best-in-class. However, they lack LATAM billing, LGPD compliance, WhatsApp integration, and prevention tools. HoliLabs's moat is the full clinical workflow, not just documentation.

key_features: ~20 second post-visit SOAP generation (fastest in market)
Unified ambient + dictation + coding + command platform
Agentic AI: initiates EHR actions, not just documentation
Nabla Connect: plug-and-play module for any EHR vendor
130+ healthcare organizations
15-point patient satisfaction increase (Denver Health study)
Navina partnership for pre-visit clinical intelligence
competitors.target_market: US health systems + European expansion (Paris HQ)

latam_presence.details: European company expanding in US market. No LATAM presence. French/English focus. No Portuguese clinical NLP.

strengths: Nabla Connect enables rapid EHR integration
European data privacy DNA (GDPR experience translates well to LGPD)
weaknesses: No LATAM market or language support
No billing code support for LATAM

threat_to_holilabs.reasoning: Nabla's European privacy-first approach and lower pricing make them a potential LATAM entrant. The Nabla Connect SDK could be adopted by LATAM EHR vendors. However, they lack full clinical workflow (billing, prevention, comms).

key_features: Ambient conversation capture with multi-speaker differentiation
37+ specialties supported
Deep Epic integration (embedded in Epic workflow)
AI actions: referral letters, after-visit summaries, evidence linking
Nursing documentation (med-surg, new 2026)
Desktop Copilot: invoke AI across apps without switching
100,000+ clinicians daily, 600+ organizations
3M+ ambient conversations per month
competitors.target_market: US + Europe health systems (expanding to Austria, France, Germany, Ireland, Belgium, Netherlands)

latam_presence.details: European expansion underway (2025-2026) but no announced LATAM plans. Microsoft Azure has LATAM presence but Dragon Copilot is not localized for LATAM healthcare.

strengths: Dragon brand recognition (30+ years in speech recognition)

threat_to_holilabs.reasoning: Pricing alone ($369-$830/month) makes Dragon Copilot inaccessible to LATAM primary care clinics. Even if Microsoft enters LATAM, they target large hospital systems, not the small/medium clinic segment HoliLabs serves.

key_features: Chart Assistant with Sage: AI chatbot in patient chart, 300+ prompts
athenaAmbient: digital scribe generating notes + diagnoses + orders from conversations (entering user testing Feb 2026)
AI-Native Clinical Encounter: real-time clinical intelligence partner
Network effects: 160,000+ providers share anonymized clinical data
athenaAmbient available at NO ADDITIONAL COST to existing customers
Supports in-person and telehealth
Advanced Intelligence Layer across entire athenaOne platform
competitors.target_market: US ambulatory care (independent practices, small-medium groups)
competitors.pricing_model: % of collections (typically 4-8% of revenue) + per-provider fees

latam_presence.details: No LATAM presence. US-only. Revenue-share pricing model doesn't translate well to LATAM payment structures.

strengths: Most relevant competitor for ambulatory/outpatient segment
athenaAmbient included FREE (massive competitive pressure on paid ambient tools)
Revenue-share pricing aligns incentives with clinics
weaknesses: athenaAmbient still in user testing (not GA as of March 2026)
No LATAM billing codes or regulatory compliance

threat_to_holilabs.reasoning: athenahealth is the closest competitor in terms of target market (ambulatory care). However, they are US-only with zero LATAM presence. Their revenue-share model and network effects don't translate to LATAM markets. The free athenaAmbient sets a pricing precedent that HoliLabs should match (ambient AI as table stakes, not premium).

holilabs_moat.summary: HoliLabs is defensible because no US competitor has built the full LATAM clinical workflow stack: LGPD compliance + TUSS/CBHPM/CUPS billing + WhatsApp patient communication + PNI/PAI vaccination tracking + RNDS/e-SUS government integration + Portuguese/Spanish clinical NLP — all at a price point accessible to LATAM primary care clinics.

differentiators.detail: TUSS, CBHPM, CUPS, RIPS, CIE-10 built into every workflow. No competitor has this.
WhatsApp is the dominant communication channel in LATAM (98% penetration in Brazil). No US competitor integrates WhatsApp natively.
Built-in LGPD Art. 33 data handling, ANVISA SaMD awareness, ANS TISS authorization. US competitors would need years to achieve this.
RNDS FHIR, e-SUS APS, SISAB, PNI vaccination tracking. No international competitor integrates with Brazilian government health systems.
Targeting $50-$150/provider/month vs $200-$830 for US competitors. 5-10x cheaper, aligned with LATAM clinic economics.
PHQ-9, GAD-7, FINDRISC, SCORE2 screening tools + government vaccination schedule tracking. No ambient-focused competitor offers this.
Portuguese + Spanish + English clinical transcription with medical terminology (Deepgram nova-3-medical). Most competitors only support English clinical NLP at full accuracy.

holilabs_moat.strategic_recommendation: HoliLabs should NOT compete on ambient documentation speed (Nabla's 20sec, Abridge's 1.5min are benchmarks to match, not beat). Instead, compete on the FULL workflow: ambient + billing + comms + prevention + government integration. Position as 'the complete clinical OS for LATAM' vs 'just another ambient scribe.' The athenahealth free-ambient precedent means ambient AI is becoming table stakes — the moat is everything around it.
